## Frequently asked questions

### Do you offer a money-back guarantee?

Yes. We offer a money-back guarantee on paid annual plans.

### What is an envelope?

An envelope is a collection of original documents. Signeasy API envelope consist of:

One or more documents/templates that has to be signed

Information about the sender

Information about the Receiver

**How can I try out the Signeasy APIs?**

Please [contact us](https://signeasy.com/api/contact-sales) and our solution expert will reach out to you within 24–48 business hours, with the details on how to provision a sandbox account for yourself to try out our APIs.

**How long are the credits valid for?**

The credits are valid for the plan's subscription period (one year).

**What is an envelope, and how are documents and envelopes related?**

At Signeasy, an envelope is a wrapper for documents that you send to a recipient to sign. An envelope can have one document or many documents, and one signer or many signers. Envelopes have statuses (i.e. sent, completed, voided) and also contain information about the sender and timestamps that indicate the progress of the delivery procedure.

Envelopes help you better manage the documents that are usually sent together and need to be signed together to complete a deal, an onboarding process, contract, etc.

Regardless of how many documents, fields, and signers a given envelope contains, it still counts as only one.

**Does Signeasy API offer an extra authentication layer at the time of signing documents?**

Yes. We support OTP authentication via SMS.

**What is the maximum number of signers supported per signature request?**

The maximum number of signers supported per signature request is 45 recipients.

**What is the maximum file size allowed per document?**

40 MB is the maximum file size allowed per document.

**Does Signeasy remind signers about signature requests sent via API?**

Yes. We remind the signers of a pending signature request for up to 7 days.

**How many apps can I create in the developer portal?**

You can create a maximum of 3 apps in the Free plan. In the Paid Plans (Starter, Intermediate and Advanced) you can create a maximum of 10 apps. If you need to increase the number of applications please reach out to our API [support team](mailto:api-support@signeasy.com).

**What happens if my plan expires?**

If the plan expires your endpoint access will be restricted and you will not be able to send any signature requests. There will be reminder notifications for plan expiry and credits utilized. Please ensure renewal of the plan ahead of time to ensure continued usage.

**Do you offer a money-back guarantee?**

Yes. We offer a money-back guarantee on paid annual plans.

**What is an envelope?**

An envelope is a collection of original documents. Signeasy API envelope consist of:

\- One or more documents/templates that has to be signed

\- Information about the sender

\- Information about the Receiver

## Key takeaways

- The best eSignature API should offer strong legal compliance (ESIGN, eIDAS, GDPR, HIPAA where needed), easy integration with REST APIs and SDKs, flexible workflows (multi-signer routing, templates, conditional logic), and built-in security features like MFA, audit trails, and encryption.

- The Top 10 eSignature APIs include Signeasy API, Adobe Acrobat Sign API, SignNow API, Dropbox Sign API, PandaDoc electronic signature API, BoldSign API, Docusign API, eSignatures.io API, Signable API, Zoho Sign API.

- eSignature API pricing usually falls into five models: Subscription-based, Pay-per-use, Tiered plans, Enterprise licensing, Free sandbox/trials.

- Use an eSignature API when you have custom workflows, need to scale document signing, or want full control over the signing experience. Use standalone eSignature software when you just need to send contracts quickly with minimal technical setup.

- Signeasy is an ideal fit for teams that need reliable, and scalable eSignature API without enterprise-level complexity or costs.

- Signeasy API offers clean RESTful APIs, detailed documentation, and SDKs for JavaScript, Python, and .NET. The API supports ESIGN, eIDAS, HIPAA, GDPR, SOC 2, 21 CFR Part 11 compliance. You’ll also get access to embedded signing, branded workflows, Webhooks, real-time tracking, and integration with popular tools like HubSpot, Google Drive, Microsoft 365.

# Top 10 eSignature APIs for teams in 2026

Finding the best [eSign API](https://signeasy.com/api)(Electronic Signature API) for your use case feels overwhelming when you're staring at a dozen providers, each seeming to be the most excellent choice:

![User asks for advice on integrating eSignature APIs with Airtable via JavaScript for client agreements](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f83f30d99580b736213bc4e/68a1ddcc3ff8a65b96576584_AD_4nXfrP7a8rtStDlIwqfKa7aJCtNRItUgL2abMS61HzFk70naM0vOgWTLcc4A-FWWS3VbnrP3QNa-eeaQZphJWIVSmwjfgxtb8MYXdaewaGnZcv3F4OPWdUPrZ6lEWs86wvkolLNQQDQ.webp)

Developers, admins, and Ops leads regularly struggle with choosing between these solutions because the stakes are high. If you pick wrong, you can be stuck with expensive per-signature costs, poor integration support, or limited customization.

To solve this problem, we've curated this list of the 10 best electronic signature APIs for 2026, which includes Signeasy, Adobe Acrobat Sign, SignNow, Dropbox Sign, PandaDoc, BoldSign, Docusign, eSignatures.io, Signable, and Zoho Sign.

### How we sourced our data

To ensure accuracy, we analyzed official API documentation, pricing pages, and developer resources from each vendor. We also reviewed customer case studies and verified claims through firsthand testing where possible.

Each option has been evaluated for ease of integration, transparent pricing, scalability, and robust feature sets.

This guide will save you research time and help you make a confident decision that aligns with your technical requirements and budget constraints.

Before we discuss why these APIs are the best, let’s define what an eSignature API is and review the key features for seamless contract management.

## What is an eSignature API?

An [eSignature API](https://signeasy.com/api)  allows businesses to embed digital signing directly into their existing platforms such as CRMs, document management systems, or custom applications.

It automates the entire process, from sending documents for signature, and tracking their progress, to securely storing signed copies, all within the same environment.

This [integration](https://signeasy.com/blog/developer/esign-integration) eliminates the need to switch between multiple apps or risk using insecure email attachments. Everything is streamlined and secure, making it easier to manage contracts, close deals, and handle legal documents efficiently.

So, what exactly makes these tools so valuable? Let’s break down the key reasons to use an eSignature API.

## Why use an eSignature API?

Managing contracts through email threads, scanned PDFs, or manual uploads is a slow and error-prone process. eSign APIs help you move past these inefficiencies. They allow you to send, sign, and manage documents directly within your own product or system.

So why use an eSignature API? It helps you work faster, stay compliant, and scale with less overhead. Some eSign API benefits include:

- Sending documents across borders instantly, without needing separate platforms or workarounds.
- Reducing turnaround time by automating tasks like signature requests, reminders, and status tracking.
- Staying aligned with legal standards like the ESIGN Act and eIDAS so you don’t run into regulatory gaps later.
- Keeping sensitive information protected with built-in audit trails, encryption, and authentication.

These aren't mere technical advantages. They are tangible business benefits that save time, reduce risk, and keep your agreements progressing without constant manual intervention.

To choose the right fit, you need to focus on features that directly impact performance and integration.

## What to look for in an eSignature API

With dozens of electronic signature APIs today, how do you separate marketing hype from genuine capability? You don't want to find out mid-project that your chosen tool lacks audit trails, is hard to integrate, or can't support multi-signer flows.

This section highlights the most important eSignature API features. These are the ones that really affect how smooth, secure, and scalable your document workflows will be.

1. Legal compliance

Make sure the API supports global standards like the ESIGN Act (U.S.), eIDAS (EU), and GDPR where needed. This is non-negotiable if you're handling customer data or international contracts. Choosing a compliant [document signer](https://signeasy.com/online-document-signer) is the first step toward building trust and ensuring the legality of your digital agreements.

2. Ease of integration

Look for well-documented SDKs, support for popular languages (JavaScript, Python, etc.), and compatibility with tools like Zapier, Airtable, Salesforce, or HubSpot.

3. Customizability

Look for APIs that let you tailor the signing experience to match your business, including branding, email templates, webhook events, and logic flows. Some providers lock you into their defaults.

4. Authentication and audit trails

Verify the API includes built-in user authentication (SMS, OTP, SSO) and detailed audit logs for accountability and security.

5. Flexible workflows

Choose an eSign APIs that handle multi-signer routing, conditional logic, and dynamic document creation.

With these key features in mind, let’s compare the leading eSignature APIs available in 2026.

## Top 10 eSignature APIs comparison

You know what to look for, but choosing the right API still isn't straightforward. Most providers check the same boxes on paper — they all have integrations, compliance certifications, and audit trails.

The hidden problem usually surfaces when you're knee-deep in implementation or trying to handle more volume than expected. To make things easier, we’ve compared 10 of the best eSignature APIs side by side.

### Best digital signature API comparison at a glance

## When to use an eSignature API vs. eSignature software

One of the most common questions we get asked is: **When should we use an eSignature API, and when is eSignature software the better option? Which one will benefit us more?** The answer depends on your specific business needs, processes, and technical capabilities.

Here’s a breakdown to help you decide:

### Use an eSignature API if:

- You have custom workflows or unique signing needs that can’t be met by standard software.
- You need to scale document signing as your business grows, and want the flexibility to integrate signing capabilities into your own systems.
- You want full control over the signing experience and interface to align with your branding.
- You need real-time tracking and audit trails directly within your internal systems.

### Use eSignature software if:

- You need a quick, easy-to-deploy solution for signing documents without requiring heavy technical setup.
- Your business processes are standard, and a ready-made solution will suffice for sending, signing, and storing documents.
- You have a small team or don’t require high levels of customization or scalability.

## Pricing models of eSignature APIs

Digital signature API cost can vary based on whether you need enterprise-grade compliance, advanced integrations, or simple document workflows. Compare [eSignature API pricing](https://signeasy.com/pricing/api) across providers to find the right fit. By understanding how their pricing works, you can avoid overpaying and confirm the solution you choose will scale with your business.

| **Subscription-based** | Flat monthly or annual fee with fixed limits on usage, users, and features. | Teams with consistent volume and workflows. |
| **Pay-per-use** | Billed per signature or document sent, no fixed monthly fees. | Small teams or low-frequency users. |
| **Tiered plans** | Different pricing tiers based on features and volume. | Growing businesses that need flexibility. |
| **Enterprise licensing** | Custom pricing with SLAs, premium support, and advanced controls. | Large orgs with complex security or volume. |
| **Free plans/trials** | Limited free usage for testing and early-stage projects. | Developers or startups evaluating APIs. |

Once you’ve found a pricing model that fits your use case, the next step is integrating the API into your workflow.

## How to integrate an eSignature API into your system

If you are embedding eSignatures into your website, mobile app, or internal HR platform, an eSignature API lets you create a fully branded signing experience and automate document workflows directly within your existing systems without starting from scratch.

Most modern eSign APIs like Signeasy's come with RESTful endpoints, clear documentation, and SDKs for languages like JavaScript, Python, and .NET. This makes it easy to integrate the eSignature API, even for low-code platforms and in-house dev teams.

Here's how eSignature API integration works:

1. **Connect your data source**: Pull customer or agreement details from your CRM or internal system.
2. **Generate the document**: Use a PDF or template. You can mail merge fields using the API to auto-fill names, dates, or pricing.
3. **Send for signature**: Trigger signature requests automatically with defined roles and authentication (OTP, SSO, etc.)
4. **Track status**: Webhooks notify your system in real-time when a document is opened, signed, or completed.

This process lets you automate manual steps like uploading, emailing, or following up. Signeasy, for example, supports CRM and cloud storage integrations out of the box, so your documents stay in sync across systems.

## Legal compliance of eSignature APIs

Different regions have different rules for what makes a signature legally binding. With the U.S. following ESIGN and the EU enforcing eIDAS, privacy laws like GDPR mean you have even more to manage. A reliable eSignature API handles all of this in the background, which means your team doesn’t have to.

### What should you know about legal compliance?

- **ESIGN Act (USA):** Validates the legality of electronic signatures across the U.S. as long as parties consent to use them and the records are retained.
- **eIDAS (EU):** Defines standards for electronic signatures in the European Union, including advanced and qualified eSignatures that meet stricter verification rules.
- **GDPR and data protection laws:** Require signers' personal information to be stored, processed, and accessed securely.

### How eSignature APIs ensure compliance

Modern APIs like Signeasy’s automatically enforce these legal standards through:

- Audit trails that log every action on the document (sent, viewed, signed, etc.).
- Multi-factor authentication (MFA) to verify signer identity.
- Timestamping and encryption to provide tamper-evident records.
- Consent tracking for all parties involved.

Signeasy, for example, is compliant with ESIGN, eIDAS, GDPR, HIPAA, and SOC 2, giving businesses the confidence to operate across borders without legal ambiguity.

From routine NDAs to industry-regulated forms like healthcare waivers, the legal checks are already in place. You don’t need extra layers of review or third-party validation — the API handles that for you.

The best eSignature solution doesn't stop at legal protection; it also simplifies the day-to-day management of your documents.

## Managing eSignatures using the API

Once documents are sent for signature, managing them at scale becomes just as important as sending them. With the right eSignature API, you can track progress, handle exceptions, and stay audit-ready without relying on email chains or external dashboards.

### How to manage eSignatures via the Signeasy API

Most APIs, including Signeasy’s, provide endpoints to help you:

- **Track document status:** Use GET /v3/documents/{document\_id} to check if a document is pending, signed, or declined.
- **View signer details:** Pull signer actions, timestamps, and IP addresses through GET /v3/documents/{document\_id}/audit\_trail.
- **Send reminders or cancel requests:** Trigger a POST request to /v3/documents/{document\_id}/remind to follow up on pending signatures.
- **Webhook support:** Subscribe to real-time updates (signed, failed, completed) using Signeasy’s webhook events.

This gives your internal systems full visibility and control, allowing for automation of everything from onboarding flows to contract renewals.

At any scale, from 10 contracts to 10,000, having live status updates and accessible audit logs means you always know where each document stands, and what’s next.

## Frequently asked questions

What is an eSignature platform?

An eSignature platform is a cloud-based service that lets users send, sign, and manage documents electronically, typically via a web interface or API. It handles signing workflows, legal compliance, authentication, storage, and tracking in one tool.

How secure are eSignature APIs?

eSignature APIs use TLS/SSL encryption, detailed audit logs, and multi-factor authentication. Many also comply with standards like SOC 2, HIPAA, eIDAS, and GDPR, making them legally admissible and secure across industries.

Which eSignature software is easiest to use?

For general ease of use, Signeasy consistently ranks high. It offers an intuitive interface, drag-and-drop signing, and minimal setup, making it ideal for non-technical users and teams needing quick deployment.

Are eSignature APIs a security risk for APIs and GDPR compliance?

No. All major eSignature APIs follow encryption standards (TLS/SSL), audit logs, and user authentication. GDPR, SOC 2, and HIPAA support is standard in enterprise-grade offerings, ensuring data protection and privacy compliance.

How long do signed documents remain legally valid?

Signed documents typically remain valid indefinitely if stored securely with audit trails and document hashes. Under eIDAS and ESIGN, signed records are admissible as evidence (even years after signing) if integrity and audit history are preserved.

## Frequently asked questions

**What are the terms and conditions of a Signeasy license?**

A Signeasy account can be used by any user, whether they are an individual or a member of a company that has purchased a paid plan. Each user must have a unique email address associated with their account. They can use their Signeasy account from multiple devices that belong to them individually. Our fair usage policy applies to each user’s account within reasonable limits of use. Signeasy reserves the right to investigate and suspend any account which violates our terms of service.

**Who is a Signeasy user?**

A user is someone who signs documents for themselves or sends documents to others for signature.

**Do the people I send documents to for signature have to pay anything?**

No, they don’t have to pay for anything. They need not even have a registered Signeasy account to sign the documents sent to them for signature.

**How will I be billed?**

All the subscriptions are billed either yearly or monthly. Your subscription will renew automatically at the end of the billing period unless you cancel the auto-renewal. If you decide to cancel, your subscription will remain active until the end of the current subscription period.

**What if I want to cancel?**

You can cancel your subscription at any time. Your account will continue to be active in the existing plan till it expires at the end of the current billing period. Owing to Signeasy’s 100-day money-back guarantee promise, no partial refunds will be provided after 100 days of your purchase (applicable on the Personal, Business, and Business Pro yearly plans only).

# signeasy business plan pricing

![Twitter](https://static.esignglobal.com/publish-assets/images/news/twitter.svg)![Facebook](https://static.esignglobal.com/publish-assets/images/news/facebook.svg)![Linkedin](https://static.esignglobal.com/publish-assets/images/news/link.svg)

## Understanding SignEasy's Business Plan Pricing in 2025

In the evolving landscape of digital transformation, electronic signature solutions like SignEasy have become essential for businesses streamlining contract workflows. From small teams to enterprises, pricing transparency is key to adoption. SignEasy's Business Plan stands out for its balance of affordability and functionality, targeting mid-sized organizations that need robust signing capabilities without enterprise-level complexity. This plan is particularly appealing for sales, HR, and legal teams handling frequent document exchanges.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251127_1938_E_signature_remix_01kb2hjjzsf75r73z64k59d5gm_7dbb6a4cd3.png)

## SignEasy Business Plan: Core Pricing Structure

SignEasy's Business Plan is priced at $15 per user per month when billed annually, totaling $180 per user per year. This tier is designed for teams requiring collaboration features beyond basic signing. Monthly billing bumps the rate to $20 per user, making the annual commitment a smarter choice for ongoing use. Unlike entry-level plans, the Business Plan removes per-envelope limits, offering unlimited envelopes—ideal for high-volume users in industries like real estate or consulting.

What sets this apart is the inclusion of unlimited templates and custom branding, allowing businesses to standardize documents while maintaining a professional look. For add-ons, features like SMS delivery or advanced integrations (e.g., with Salesforce or Google Workspace) incur extra fees: SMS authentication is $0.50 per message, and premium integrations start at $5 per user monthly. This modular approach helps control costs based on actual needs, avoiding overpayment for unused tools.

From a business perspective, this pricing reflects SignEasy's focus on scalability. A team of 10 users would pay $1,800 annually for the core plan, with potential savings through volume discounts for 50+ seats (negotiable via sales). Envelope tracking is unlimited, but automation sends—like bulk operations—are capped at 500 per month per user to prevent abuse, aligning with fair-use policies seen across the industry.

## Features Breakdown: What You Get with SignEasy Business

Diving deeper, the Business Plan includes team workspaces for shared access, real-time notifications, and audit trails compliant with standards like eIDAS and ESIGN Act. Users can embed signing requests in emails or apps, with mobile support for on-the-go approvals. Conditional routing—where documents route based on prior responses—enhances workflow efficiency, a boon for complex approvals in procurement or partnerships.

Security features include two-factor authentication and role-based permissions, ensuring data integrity without add-ons. Integration ecosystem is strong, supporting over 50 apps, from Microsoft 365 to HubSpot, reducing silos in tech stacks. For global operations, SignEasy offers multi-language support and timestamping, though advanced identity verification (e.g., biometrics) requires upgrading to Enterprise at custom pricing.

Cost-wise, businesses should factor in implementation: onboarding is free for the first month, but custom API access for developers adds $300 annually per integration. Overall, the plan's value proposition shines for SMBs—affordable entry with room to grow, averaging 20-30% lower total cost of ownership compared to legacy paper-based processes, per industry benchmarks.

## Limitations and Scalability Considerations

While competitive, SignEasy's Business Plan has limits. It supports up to 100 users per account before Enterprise kicks in, and high-volume API calls (over 10,000 monthly) trigger overage fees at $0.10 per envelope. In APAC regions, occasional latency in document rendering can affect user experience, though updates in 2025 aim to address this. For enterprises needing SSO or advanced analytics, the jump to custom pricing starts at $25 per user monthly.

Business observers note that while SignEasy excels in ease-of-use, it may not match the depth of enterprise-grade compliance in regulated sectors like finance. Still, for 80% of mid-market needs, it delivers solid ROI, with users reporting 40% faster signing cycles.

## Comparing SignEasy with Leading Competitors

To evaluate SignEasy's positioning, it's useful to benchmark against established players. The electronic signature market, valued at over $5 billion in 2025, emphasizes compliance, integration, and cost-efficiency. Below is a neutral comparison table highlighting key aspects across DocuSign, Adobe Sign, eSignGlobal, and others like HelloSign (now Dropbox Sign). This analysis draws from public pricing data and focuses on business plans for fairness.

| Feature/Aspect | SignEasy Business | DocuSign Business Pro | Adobe Sign Business | eSignGlobal Essential | HelloSign (Dropbox Sign) Business |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Annual Price per User** | $180 | $480 | $360 (billed annually) | $200 (approx. $16.6/month) | $240 |
| **Envelope Limit** | Unlimited | ~100/year | Unlimited | 100/month | Unlimited (with fair use) |
| **User Seats** | Up to 100 | Up to 50 (scalable) | Unlimited | Unlimited | Unlimited |
| **Key Features** | Templates, mobile signing, integrations | Bulk send, payments, conditional logic | Acrobat integration, forms | Global compliance (100+ countries), API flexibility | Simple UI, Dropbox sync |
| **Add-Ons (e.g., SMS/IDV)** | $0.50/msg; custom | Metered (~$1/msg) | Included in higher tiers | Low-cost regional auth | Basic, extra fees |
| **APAC/Regional Strengths** | Good mobile support | Higher latency, surcharges | Strong US/EU focus | Native APAC integration (e.g., Singpass) | Limited regional compliance |
| **Best For** | SMBs, quick setups | Complex workflows | PDF-heavy users | Cross-border APAC | Creative teams |
| **Overall Value** | High affordability | Feature-rich but pricey | Seamless with Adobe ecosystem | Cost-effective compliance | User-friendly basics |

This table underscores trade-offs: SignEasy offers strong value for unlimited use, while competitors vary in specialization.

### DocuSign: The Industry Standard

DocuSign's Business Pro plan, at $40 per month per user annually ($480/year), caters to teams needing advanced automation like bulk sends and payment collection. It includes ~100 envelopes per user yearly, with add-ons for identity verification adding metered costs. Strengths lie in global brand trust and integrations, but APAC users face higher effective pricing due to compliance surcharges and latency issues. For businesses prioritizing robustness over cost, it's a safe bet, though scalability often requires custom negotiations.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251127_1927_Docusign_simple_compose_01kb2gzcxjf90s2d849a4yzkes_f4a40eeee0.png)

### Adobe Sign: Integrated PDF Powerhouse

Adobe Sign's Business plan starts at $30 per month per user ($360/year), emphasizing seamless ties to Acrobat for PDF editing and forms. Unlimited envelopes make it suitable for document-intensive workflows, with built-in e-sign compliance across US and EU. However, API access and advanced auth come at premiums, and it's less optimized for non-Adobe ecosystems. Ideal for creative or legal firms already in the Adobe suite, but pricing can escalate with add-ons like SMS delivery.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251127_1941_Adobe_simple_compose_01kb2httnmftqttna4mq0d3mrb_3366684d27.png)

### eSignGlobal: Regional Compliance Focus

eSignGlobal positions itself as a compliant alternative, supporting electronic signatures in over 100 mainstream countries and regions globally. In the Asia-Pacific, it holds advantages with optimized performance, lower pricing, and native integrations—such as seamless connectivity with Hong Kong's iAM Smart and Singapore's Singpass for identity verification. The Essential plan, for instance, costs just $16.6 per month (billed annually), allowing up to 100 documents for signature, unlimited user seats, and verification via access codes. This delivers exceptional value on compliance foundations, often 20-30% cheaper than peers for similar features. For more details on [eSignGlobal pricing](https://www.esignglobal.com/pricing), businesses can explore tailored options that prioritize regional data residency and reduced latency.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/Im_smart_cc2801eb55.png)

### Other Competitors: HelloSign and Beyond

HelloSign (now under Dropbox) offers a straightforward Business plan at $20 per month ($240/year), with unlimited envelopes and easy Dropbox integration—great for collaborative file sharing. It lacks deep automation but excels in simplicity. PandaDoc, another contender, blends proposals with signing at $19 per user monthly, suiting sales teams. Overall, these options fill niches but may require supplements for global scale.

## Strategic Insights for Businesses Choosing an e-Signature Solution

Selecting an e-signature provider involves weighing pricing against compliance, usability, and regional needs. SignEasy's Business Plan provides a cost-effective entry for growing teams, but factors like APAC operations or advanced integrations might favor alternatives. In regulated markets, ensuring alignment with local laws—such as GDPR or PDPA—is paramount.

As a neutral observer, for DocuSign users seeking alternatives with strong regional compliance, eSignGlobal emerges as a viable option, particularly for Asia-Pacific efficiency without compromising global standards. Businesses are encouraged to trial plans and assess based on specific workflows.

FAQs

What is the pricing for SignEasy's Business Plan?

SignEasy's Business Plan is priced at $15 per user per month when billed annually, or $20 per user per month when billed monthly. This plan is designed for organizations requiring advanced features such as team management and integrations.

What features are included in the SignEasy Business Plan?

The Business Plan includes unlimited envelopes, team collaboration tools, custom branding, API access, and priority support. It supports up to 100 users and provides compliance with standards like GDPR and eIDAS.

How does SignEasy's Business Plan compare to its Pro Plan in terms of pricing and features?

The Business Plan costs more than the Pro Plan, which is $10 per user per month annually, but offers additional enterprise-level features like advanced reporting, SSO integration, and bulk sending capabilities not available in the Pro Plan.
